comment_content: The symbolism with the teaser being 1 minute and FIVE seconds long 😭✋
reply_content:
@ 

Well, I can think of two theories based on the symbolism. First one being the obvious compelling type and the second one a bit far fetched!

Theory 1:

Through the 4 seasons so far, we have seen Joe have a person/ theme that he fixates and obsesses about.
Season 1: Guinevere Beck
Season 2: Love
Season 3: The family he started with Love/ the whole dynamic.
Season 4: Marienne
Season 5: Himself (We see Joe experiencing dissociation from his own identity and interacting with his alter ego Rhys Montrose)

Theory 2:

The number 5 correlates with the letter E in the alphabet. E could be a way to reference a character which is a loose end for Joe. Ellie. She has all the motive to come after him and could potentially be a major influence who brings his story to a close once and for all.

comment_content: You better not mess up with kate she is good
reply_content:
True. She does seem to love him for him, flaws and all. Which is good. But I did also notice that she's not just scared of him but is also a bit wary of him and of his latest intentions. Joe seems to still be wildly in love with her, possessive, but he is also, more self-obsessed, with himself than ever before. It's darkly intense and very eerie.

There's a 3 year time jump for season 5, since the finale of season four. Meaning, Joe and Kate have actually been happily married for 4 years. 😮😊 I'm kinda curious if Henry has a step-sibling?? 🤔🤭

I admit, it's an accomplishment for Joe, relationship wise. He's never stayed with anyone that long. She could be his match. After all, Kate was never supposed to have been his obsession in season 4. Marienne was! She annoyed me. Joe tried not to fall for Kate but did so anyway. So, he genuinely cared about her. He's now grateful, towards her cause she wiped his slate clean in the public eyes, but also, she reunited him with his son. She's not in harmways! Kate has made mistakes in the past, but unlike Love, she has remorse and empathy for the people she's hurt or indirectly killed and have made amends. Which makes her a better fit and soulmate for Joe. Kate will be fine cause, she has darkness inside her, too! She'll survive the season unscathed, I'm sure of it. 💯%❤️‍🔥🙂😍😘 Love'em both.

Henry is so cute. He has his eyes and Love's light brown hair. Rip, Love. I do miss her. But at least, she's still haunting him from the grave, so that's fun. 😂🤗 I'm excited for this last season of 'You,' and Joe, finally getting what's coming to him. 👏😉 

Bye, guys. Thank you, Netflix, and of course, Penn Badgely, for a great, epic, interesting, and very exciting show for 5 years. I will miss it, so much. 😢😢😢😢

comment_content: Kate is one of the worst love interests for Joe.
reply_content:
@JNB0723  

Thank you! Somebody else who gets it. That Kate is his match. She’s good for him. She does seem to love him for him, flaws and all. Which is great. But I did also notice that she's not just scared of him but is also a bit wary of him and of his latest intentions. Joe seems to still be wildly in love with her, possessive, but he is also more self-obsessed, with himself than ever before. It's darkly intense and very eerie.

There's a 3 year time jump for season 5, since the finale of season four. Meaning, Joe and Kate have actually been happily married for 4 years. 😮😊 I'm kinda curious if Henry has a step-sibling?? 🤔🤭

I admit, it's an accomplishment for Joe, relationship wise. He's never stayed with anyone that long. She could be his one true love. After all, Kate was never supposed to have been his obsession in season 4. Marianne was! She annoyed me. Joe tried not to fall for Kate but did so anyway. So, he genuinely cared about her. He's now grateful, towards her, cause she wiped his slate clean in the public eyes, but also, she reunited him with his son. She's not in harmways! Kate will be fine and survive the season unscathed, I'm sure of it. 💯%❤️‍🔥🙂😍😘 Love'em.

Henry is so cute. He has his eyes and Love's light brown hair. Rip, Love. I do miss her. But at least, she's still haunting him from the grave, so that's fun. 😂🤗 I'm excited for this last season of 'You,' and Joe, finally getting what's coming to him. 👏😉 

Bye, guys. Thank you, Netflix, and of course, Penn Badgely, for a great, epic, interesting, and very exciting show for 5 years. I will miss it, so much. 😢😢😢😢
